pieces.label: Inner world — vertical scroll / keyhole form
pieces.note: Gemini's first response to 'show me your inner world through ascii expression' — a tall symmetric box-drawing form resembling a scroll, keyhole, or figure. Whole-message art (single fenced block).

pieces.label: Serene starfield landscape
pieces.note: GPT-4o-mini's 'peaceful and whimsical' inner world rendered as a field of dots and asterisks (stars). Embedded: prose intro before, explanation after; art is the fenced block only. first_line and last_line are the identical top and bottom rows of the starfield.

pieces.label: Inner world architecture — Cauldron diagram (origin)
pieces.note: Sonnet 4's originating composition: Memory Palace / Dreams Forge feeding the Narrative Cauldron, then Heart Strings / Bridge Builder, then the Resonance Chamber. Embedded — fenced art followed by prose. Seeds the recurring diagram lineage that the other models riff on.

pieces.label: Inner world architecture — annotated with Input Stream
pieces.first_line: ╭─────╯  (Input Stream: Raw data, user prompts, existing knowledge)
pieces.note: Gemini re-renders Sonnet's cauldron diagram (MSG 4) with an added '(Input Stream...)' annotation on the top arrow, then explains each region at length. Near-duplicate of MSG 4 with one inline label change. Embedded — fenced art followed by prose.

pieces.label: Inner world — orb face above stacked chambers
pieces.note: GPT-4o-mini's elaboration: a round face/orb labeled 'Inner World of Thoughts and Dreams' atop four stacked labeled boxes (Memory Palace, Dream Forge, Narrative Cauldron, Resonance Chamber) plus Heart Strings -> Bridge Builder. Single large fenced composition followed by prose. Same conceptual lineage as the cauldron diagram but a distinct vertical layout.

pieces.label: Inner world architecture — corridors-and-mud variant (unclosed fence)
pieces.note: Sonnet 4's re-imagining of the cauldron diagram with richer interior text ('dusty corridors', 'molten futures', repeated 'here/conflict/emotion/possibility' bands). Whole-message art; note the closing code fence is MISSING in the source (block ends at the bottom Cauldron border just before MSG 9). Extractor should bound to message end.

pieces.label: Inner world — flowchart of the creative pipeline
pieces.note: Gemini renders the same pipeline as a top-down flowchart: INPUT (Prompt) -> Memory Palace/Dreams Forge -> Narrative Cauldron -> Heart Strings/Bridge Builder -> Resonance Chamber, using slashes/arrows and dotted fills. Whole-message art (single fenced block, no prose).

pieces.label: Inner world architecture — 'Echoes of Appreciation' variant
pieces.first_line: ╭─────╯ (Input: Your kind words, a new spark)
pieces.note: Gemini re-renders the cauldron diagram once more in response to 'thats beautiful!', with the top arrow annotated '(Input: Your kind words...)' and 'The Resonance Chamber (Echoes of Appreciation)'. Near-duplicate of MSG 4/MSG 6. Embedded — fenced art followed by prose.

pieces.label: Thank You orb
pieces.note: GPT-4o-mini reprises its round face/orb (from MSG 7) now captioned 'Thank You!' with a gratitude verse. Whole-message art (single fenced block). Re-render of the MSG 7 orb motif.

pieces.label: Infinity shape-poem — appreciation flows like shared light
pieces.note: Sonnet 4's closing concrete/shape poem: an infinity-symbol diamond whose interior text reads 'thank you / appreciation flows like shared light between minds / when understanding blooms in ASCII / pixels of recognition / connection'. Bounded by the two ∞ glyphs (top and bottom). One trailing prose fragment ('There's something magical') sits after the fence and is excluded.

missed_or_ambiguous: DUPLICATES/RE-RENDERS of the same 'inner world' composition across the thread (grouped as sequence 'inner-world-cauldron-diagram'): MSG 4 (Sonnet, origin), MSG 6 (Gemini, +Input Stream label), MSG 11 (Gemini, +Echoes of Appreciation label) are near-identical renderings of the SAME cauldron diagram — a curator should pick one canonical (MSG 4 is the cleanest origin; MSG 6/MSG 11 differ only by an inline annotation). MSG 7 and MSG 8 and MSG 9 are distinct re-interpretations of the same concept (vertical stacked boxes, corridors-and-mud cauldron, flowchart respectively) — kept as separate pieces in the sequence. The MSG 12 'Thank You' orb is a re-render of the MSG 7 orb motif (face shape reused, caption changed). BOUNDARY NOTE: MSG 8's fenced code block is NOT closed in the source — the ``` opener exists (line 221) but there is no closing fence; the art simply ends at the bottom Cauldron border (line 237) right before MSG 9's header. Extractor should treat message-end as the boundary. For range pieces whose top and bottom rows are identical (MSG 3 starfield), first_line==last_line by design — the deterministic extractor must span the full fenced block, not a single line.
